The waiting area was also really warm by the way. No one even gave us a menu or tried to take an advance order at least. We ended up leaving in frustration. We often come here for lunch. author: HYERAN KIM
text: Is the good review just because there aren't many Korean BBQ restaurants in the center of Sydney? I ordered two servings of pork ribs and two servings of pork belly, but most of it was fat, so I could barely eat any of it. Once it was grilled, the condition was much worse than I had expected, so I eventually spoke to the part-time staff member. I wasn't asking for a replacement, but simply wanted to point out that the meat quality was very poor. The staff member said they would speak to the owner, but from a distance, I saw the owner shake their head after hearing it. Seeing that really hurt my feelings. Even when paying, I mentioned that I couldn't eat much because the meat quality was so bad, but I was told, "If you had told us in advance, we would have replaced it for you." I wasn't even trying to ask for an exchange in the first place. I had been missing Korean food for months and had really high expectations for this day, as it was the first time in a long while I was eating proper pork belly. However, I was very disappointed by both the quality of the meat and the service. Additionally, we changed the grill plate a total of six times while eating four servings of meat. That is how easily it burns and sticks.

text: It was absolutely awful, not authentic at all, even worse than Taiwanese sesame noodles. It had no aroma whatsoever, and the sauce they served didn't have any minced meat. A bowl of noodles cost 200 NTD! You had to add the sweet bean sauce yourself to make it taste even remotely authentic. Don't be fooled by the photos of Baek Jong-won at the entrance; this sesame noodle dish was practically a half-finished product. Even the sweet and sour chicken sauce tasted the same as Taiwanese sweet and sour pork ribs, just with a slightly different frying powder.

There were only about 2-3 staff members at the front of house, and they seemed a bit overwhelmed as they were constantly helping to prepare the ingredients. Both the cold and hot soups were well-seasoned, but the pork was disappointing; it lacked meaty flavor (perhaps the beef was better?). The lettuce platter was beautiful, and the steamed egg was photogenic, but the seasoning wasn't particularly memorable.

author: Janie
text: First visit to the restaurant! ❤️ I've always wanted to eat there since I knew it was opened by Baek Jong-won, but unfortunately, I couldn't get a reservation. Today I finally had the chance to try it, and it definitely lived up to my expectations! 😍 ✅ The beef and pork had no gamey smell. I highly recommend the beef and Matsusaka pork! They were really delicious! 👍🏽 I was amazed by the doneness and texture of the meat grilled by the staff. ✅ The side dishes and platters were very generous in portion size. There were various vegetables to wrap the meat in, and the side dishes were also very tasty and plentiful, so you didn't have to keep ordering more. ✅ The Busan-style seafood pancake, although not my preferred crispy texture, was made with high-quality ingredients. The seafood was very fresh, and one serving was very filling. I recommend sharing it with others. ✅ They even have mini portions of cold noodles! Wanting cold noodles but worried about being too full? They actually have mini portions! 😆 How thoughtful! ✅The food smelled wonderful after the meal! I'm usually afraid of smelling like grease after eating barbecue. But this time, my clothes didn't smell at all after I went out. I was quite surprised by that! 🤩 In short, I had a very enjoyable meal! I will definitely come back!

author: Alex Lim
text: Honestly one of the best Korean BBQ spots around. The quality of the meat is fantastic—the marinated pork belly and beef short ribs are incredibly juicy and have that perfect charcoal-grilled finish. You also have to get one of their stews; the 7-minute pork and kimchi stew is thick, rich, and perfect poured over steam rice. author: Chi Ho Albert Liem
text: Melbourne's Culinary Landmark | BORNGA: The Soul of Korean BBQ by Chef Baek Jong-won, a True Feast on the Charcoal Grill In Melbourne, a culinary capital where diverse cultures converge, Korean BBQ has always been a vibrant and competitive scene. However, for those seeking the most authentic and representative flavors of Korean cuisine, the city center's **BORNGA** is undoubtedly an irreplaceable pilgrimage site for countless gourmets. As the flagship chain brand of renowned Korean national treasure chef Baek Jong-won, BORNGA Melbourne perfectly replicates the ultimate flavors of Seoul, showcasing a unique culinary aesthetic in everything from the decor and charcoal grilling to the selection of ingredients. I. Stepping into a Space of Art: A Korean Aesthetic Blending Tradition and Modernity Upon entering the restaurant, the impressive "BORNGA" sign, accompanied by a lightbox featuring Chef Baek Jong-won's warm smile, immediately catches the eye, as if assuring every diner of the authenticity and high quality of this culinary journey. Stepping inside, the spacious and grand ambiance is immediately striking. The most eye-catching feature is the high-rising traditional Korean tiled roof, paired with a calming wood tone and clean, warm lighting, instantly creating an elegant atmosphere reminiscent of a traditional Korean mansion. Each table is equipped with a signature bronze-colored, powerful smoke extraction pipe, not only aesthetically pleasing but also providing excellent smoke removal, allowing diners to enjoy their grilling comfortably and elegantly without worrying about being covered in smoke afterwards. A carefully arranged photo wall documents the brand's history and Chef Baek's dedication to culinary excellence, adding a rich cultural depth to the restaurant. II. The Soul of Grilled Meat: Authentic High-Temperature Solid Wood Charcoal For authentic Korean barbecue, the charcoal fire is crucial to success. This restaurant insists on using high-quality solid wood charcoal. When the server brings the glowing red-hot charcoal grill to the table, the subtle aroma of charcoal foreshadows the delicious meal to come. Compared to ordinary electric or gas grills, the high temperature of charcoal instantly locks in the juices of the meat and leaves an irresistible smoky aroma on the surface. This is the secret to "Benjia's" ability to maintain the tenderness and juiciness of its meat. III. Abundant Korean Side Dishes (Banchan): A Sincere Prelude to the Taste Buds Before the main courses arrive, Benjia's dazzling array of free, unlimited side dishes (Banchan) is enough to impress. These side dishes are anything but perfunctory: Freshly Juiced Vegetable Salad: Crisp and sweet vegetables paired with a special milky-white dressing, incredibly refreshing and appetizing. Pa-muchim (Scallion Salad): With a hint of spiciness and the aroma of sesame oil, it's the perfect accompaniment to grilled meat, cutting through the richness and enhancing the flavor. Smooth and Creamy Potato Salad: With a delicate and smooth texture, sweet but not cloying. Authentic Korean Kimchi: Perfectly balanced in sour and spicy flavors, fermented to perfection, it's excellent whether eaten alone or grilled. IV. Culinary Highlights: A Feast of Meat Platters and Signature Dishes 1. Deluxe Assorted BBQ Platter The meats, presented on a sturdy round wooden platter, are a feast for the eyes. The distinct layers of meat showcase the exceptionally high standards of ingredient selection: * **Signature Woo Samgyeop:** This is Chef Baek Jong-won's personally developed and patented star product! Ultra-thin slices of beef brisket are drizzled with the restaurant's special secret sauce. Cooked for just a few seconds in the pan, it melts in your mouth, the subtle sweetness of the sauce and the creamy aroma of the beef creating a truly exquisite flavor. * **Thick-Cut Fresh Pork Belly (Samgyeopsal):** A perfect balance of fat and lean meat, grilled to a golden-brown crisp by the charcoal fire. Excess fat drips into the charcoal, releasing a rich aroma, while the meat itself is tender and chewy. Premium Marbled Beef: Boasting perfectly marbled fat, the meat is a vibrant pink. After being grilled over charcoal, the juices are completely locked inside, bursting in your mouth with pure beef flavor the moment you bite into it. Thoughtful Details: The platter also includes freshly sliced ​​pumpkin, shiitake mushrooms, and a king oyster mushroom specially branded with "Benjia BORNGA," adding a visually appealing touch and making it even more juicy and flavorful after grilling. 2. Soulful Side Dish: Gyeran-jjim (Volcano Steamed Egg) This Korean steamed egg, served in a traditional black clay pot, is an absolute must-order! The egg rises like a volcanic eruption, incredibly fluffy and smooth, still steaming when you scoop it with a spoon. Rich in egg flavor, topped with fresh scallions, it's delicate like a soufflé, with a subtle savory broth flavor that melts in your mouth—warm and comforting. 3. Classic Soup Dishes: Soybean Paste Stew and Tofu Stew While enjoying your barbecue, a sip of piping hot, rich tofu stew or Korean soybean paste stew is a perfect finishing touch. The tender tofu and the savory seafood or beef blend seamlessly in the slightly spicy broth, not only cutting through the richness but also providing a perfect ending to your meal. Summary Review BORNGA in Melbourne not only weaves a mouthwatering culinary map, but it's also a brilliant presentation of the craftsmanship of Korean cuisine. From high-quality charcoal and patented signature meats to impeccable service and a captivating dining environment, it perfectly embodies the meaning of "Original Korean Taste." Whether it's a gathering with friends, a family birthday celebration, or simply a treat for your taste buds, BORNGA offers a feast for both the eyes and the palate. If you're in Melbourne, this is definitely a top-tier Korean BBQ restaurant worth visiting again and again! Recommendation rating: ★★★★★ (5/5) Must-try dishes: Signature Woo Samgyeop, Deluxe Meat Platter, Steamed Egg with Volcano

author: sungae lee
text: A few years ago, I took my parents to Bon Ga in Phnom Penh and was greatly disappointed. Since I was visiting Sihanouk after a long time, I stopped by hoping to see a Bon Ga there. As expected, Bon Ga did not disappoint... The Bossam was half fat, and the smell was unbelievable. After ordering a few dishes, I looked at the side dish bowls; in the stir-fried anchovies, there was something that looked like hair—I don't know what kind of hair it was—but it suddenly made me feel nauseous. The staff taking orders couldn't even take the order properly; it was frustrating. Just as there are people who smoke in restaurants, there are also inconsiderate people these days who smoke in restaurants. Also, seeing ashtrays on the tables in restaurants... I know Baek Jong-won is the owner, but it seems that instead of just expanding overseas and increasing the number of stores, they need much more thorough management. It is so disappointing. It feels like a waste of money, and it is extremely unsanitary. Even one star is too much, but I can't register a review unless I check the box. Damn it...

text: Friday 12 June 2026, around 19.00-20.00, We ate there. We ordered Yangnyeom Daeji Galbi 168,000 idr (pork meat). And then the waiter sent us cooked Yangnyeom Galbi around 460,000 idr (beef meat). At first we thought that we were wrong in giving information to the waiter. And then the waiter sent us again the cooked Yangnyeom Daeji Galbi again , so the order become 2 dishes. The manager of Bornga Bandung (Male Manager using different uniform) Giving us solution to pay the Yangnyeom Galbi since we had already eaten the Beef dishes. Even though it was the Resto team whom doing the wrong order. But the surprise , we still need to pay the Yangnyeom Daeji Galbi. So we need to pay both dishes, otherwise the waiter who were giving the wrong dishes need to pay that dishes. Of course we do not want the waiter to pay. It needs to be the restaurant as a team who were responsible for this problem. We give 1 rating for the service because the management is not really wise in order to handle problem. The Wrong Order by Restaurant team , but the customer pay all ... Very "Nice" Service... Nice in terms of very very poor service

author: Jung wie alt
text: The sweet and sour pork was delicious. However, for the Jjamppong, the meat wasn't properly thawed before frying, so the pieces were stuck together. Also, when using the sirloin typically used for sweet and sour pork in Jjamppong, it is usually chopped into smaller pieces; these were too large, which was disappointing. (Since sirloin has less fat, it is usually used for deep-frying and is not typically chopped thickly for soups.) Wouldn't it have been better to use thin slices of beef brisket or pork belly instead... If they were going to use the sirloin from sweet and sour pork directly in Jjamppong, they should have prepared the ingredients much better... Also, the mussels in the Jjamppong were in a wretched state. They could have added two or three large green-shell mussels and a few pieces of the sliced ​​squid sold at Asian markets; the cost ratio wouldn't have been that high. It was a pity that they tried to cut costs by extracting too much vegetable broth to achieve flavor, resulting in a complete breakdown of all the details. I wish they would stick to a clear concept and recipe, whether it's meat jjamppong or seafood jjamppong. Since they employ foreign staff and can't replicate the authentic Korean taste, I think it would be tastier to use proper ingredients and make it easier by using artificial oils or powders. Even considering it's overseas... the quality of the jjamppong is far too low for the price. Also, the facility issues are significant. As soon as I entered, the sewer smell ruined my appetite, and throughout the meal, the sound of a sewage treatment machine running on the left wall was constant... I honestly felt that this wasn't a suitable location for a restaurant. I actually debated whether or not to post this review. Whether it gets deleted or I delete it later, I hope this honest review reaches the management and helps the restaurant improve and prosper. It is unfortunate that the current quality and the smell issues will create a very negative perception among the diverse range of customers seeking Korean food.

text: My friends and I's favorite hangout spot. The meat is always fresh, juicy, yet tender. My favorites are the samgyeopsal and hangjungsal. We also always order the soup, sundubu jjigae. It's savory and refreshing, a must-have accompaniment to any meal. The banchan (side dishes) are also delicious, with a wide variety, and refillable options. For dessert, we like to have yogurt ice cream. It's not too sweet and refreshing, with a slight sourness. It's totally worth the price. Keep it up, Saemaeul!

author: SS DD
text: Saemaeul at Central Rama 9 is a great choice for Korean barbecue lovers. The quality of both the pork and beef was impressive, with tender textures and rich flavors that made every bite enjoyable. One of the best parts of the meal was the wide variety of side dishes, which could be refilled throughout the dining experience, adding great value and variety. The soup was flavorful and comforting, pairing perfectly with the grilled meats. I also enjoyed the rice balls, which were tasty and fun to eat alongside the barbecue. The service was excellent from start to finish. The staff were attentive, friendly, and helped grill the meat at the table, ensuring everything was cooked perfectly without any hassle. Overall, Saemaeul delivers delicious Korean food, generous side dishes, and professional service, making it a highly enjoyable dining experience in Bangkok.

author: Sonrisa
text: We came here expecting to find a restaurant owned by a famous Korean chef, but we were quite disappointed. Some dishes weren't even decent, and the overall price was too high. Jajangmyeon (Black Bean Noodles): On the sweeter side, it was generally tasty. Seafood Stir-fried Noodles: The noodles were incredibly salty (to an unbelievable degree to both me and my companions). After we told the staff, they responded quickly and made us a new one. The new noodles tasted better, but the toppings, including wood ear mushrooms and shellfish, were still extremely salty. I don't know if it's due to the "special" way the ingredients were prepared, but many of them were meant to be enjoyed raw or to enhance the broth. We could only taste the salt, not the broth. Neither of us are particularly fond of bland food; we usually enjoy strong flavors and varied dishes, but we all agreed that this dish was just too salty. Sweet and Sour Pork: The texture was decent, chewy, and sweet and sour—above average. Korean Fried Chicken: Nothing special, nothing special. Side dishes: The raw onions seemed to have been treated to reduce their pungent smell, but they were still a bit strong. The yellow radishes were quite good, sweet and sour, not salty. The restaurant was clean and bright, and because it wasn't crowded, it was also spacious, making it a great place to enjoy a leisurely meal and chat with family and friends if you're interested in trying something new.

author: Clem
text: I've been to the one in the city and had a pretty good experience but the one at Rhodes wasn't as good. The meat was not bad and the self serve for side dishes is great - but that's about it. We ordered a lot of the menu outside of the meat section and the food was pretty average, I think I expected a bit more out of Bornga because of the brand. The beef yukhoe was actually so bad though - the meat was like so cold that it was like it just came out of the freezer and it wasn't even seasoned. I definitely expect the meat dishes to be on point so it was really disappointing. The service was ok on the most part but it's definitely not good service - we even had to wait to pay because there's only one person at the counter who deals with both the customers who have just arrived and those who are trying to pay. And they have this thing where the person at the front will walk the customers in to the table so if there are multiple customers waiting to come in, you're basically just watching the counter person back and forth until it's your turn - and I'm just trying to pay... Anyways, it's ok overall butfor premium pricing, I expect good food and service and neither of them hit the mark.

author: Nandor Farkas
text: I'm not Korean, so I can't judge the food from a local's perspective. Nevertheless, I was extremely impressed by the taste. I've visited numerous Korean-Chinese restaurants in various European countries, but this one was truly special and completely different from anything I've experienced before. From the outside and inside, the restaurant appears rather simple and spartan. Ordering and payment are done via QR code directly at the table. Therefore, I didn't initially have particularly high expectations. However, what was served completely surprised me. The entire concept is simply ingenious. Truly impressive! The taste of the food is practically etched in my memory. In fact, we came back the next day to eat there – and it was just as excellent as the day before. Anyone wanting to enjoy Korean-Chinese cuisine in Amsterdam should definitely go there. We'll be back! 🍜😊

author: Charlotte Pat
text: This is my go-to restaurant in Korea, but I'd never tried it in Thailand until today. Today was my first time. It tasted just as delicious as always. The restaurant seated people in the back, so it looked empty, but it was actually quite crowded. I'm a little confused why they seated everyone in the back, making the restaurant seem quiet. (I'd recommend seating people in the front; it would make it look more inviting.) The staff were incredibly friendly and always smiling. They grilled every plate of pork for us, so we could eat comfortably. Side dishes were unlimited. Most importantly, the smoke extraction system was excellent; my clothes and hair didn't smell like smoke like at other BBQ restaurants. 10/10!

author: K K
text: Very disappointing experience. 🫥🫥 We ordered at 7:05 PM and received most of our food by 7:30 PM, which was understandable given how busy the restaurant was. However, one of our dishes (jjamppong) never arrived while tables seated AFTER us received ALL of their food. We asked about it after around 40 minutes and were told they would check with the kitchen, but NO update was provided. After nearly 50 minutes, the dish finally arrived with no explanation. Only when we questioned the delay were we told that our order had been missed. Mistakes happen, but if the order had been forgotten, we should have been informed and apologized to when we first asked. What disappointed us most was not the mistake itself, but the lack of communication and accountability. We were simply told they were busy, and when we asked to speak to a manager, we were told there wasn't one available. 1. 짬뽕 나오는데 50분 걸림 + 중간에 물어봤을 때 상황 설명 없이 그냥 본인 할 일 하심… 2. 기본 반찬 양파 한개+ 춘장 한방울 나와요 3. 면이 거의 수제비면

author: Monica “몬몬스타”
text: I’ve visited the Sydney branch multiple times, and they always accepted reservations for two people. Even when I arrived a bit late once, they were still welcoming and kind. However, the Adelaide branch was a completely different experience. They don’t allow online reservations for two people at all, and when I tried calling, that didn’t work either. Their notice says that parties of two are walk‑in only, so I went as instructed.but when I arrived, they told me there were no seats available. What made it worse was that they offered to book a reservation for another day for people who were already inside the restaurant, while turning me away. I honestly don’t understand how this policy works. I’m not sure if this is a rule from the franchise or just this specific Adelaide location, but it was disappointing. I was planning to bring my Australian friends here if the food was good, but after this experience, I’d rather go back to the Sydney branch instead. Really unfortunate.

text: Had dinner at Paik’s BBQ and it definitely lived up to the hype. 🥩🔥 The meat was fresh, well-marinated, and grilled perfectly at the table. The side dishes kept coming, and every dish felt comforting and full of flavour. Service was quick and attentive even during a busy dinner rush. What I loved most was how family-friendly the atmosphere was—lively enough to feel authentic, but still comfortable for dining with kids. If you’re craving Korean BBQ that’s delicious, satisfying, and great value for money, Paik’s BBQ is absolutely worth a visit. We left full, happy, and already talking about what we’d order next time.

author: Chiu Joshua
text: This is my first time writing a Google restaurant review, and I never expected it to be such a negative one. I dined here a year ago and thought the stir-fried noodles were delicious, so I've always wanted to go back. After eating here today, I was extremely surprised. The portion of noodles was noticeably smaller, and they only gave me two mussels, one of which was empty, along with three small squid rings. This is the amount of food they served... for $210. As someone who works in the consumer business, I understand the costs and prices for businesses, but I never imagined that the quality of food in a typical Taipei restaurant would deteriorate to this extent...

author: Choong Joanne
text: Very disappointing dining experience. The jajangmyeon was not good at all. The sauce tasted bland and unpleasant, and it was one of the worst versions I've had. The seafood soup was even worse. The broth had a very strong, unpleasant seafood smell and taste, making it difficult to enjoy. It felt as if some of the seafood was not fresh, which affected the entire soup. One of the shellfish also had a powdery, chalky texture that tasted strange and unpleasant. What shocked us the most was finding what appeared to be mold on the sliced garlic (or garnish) served on top of the fried chicken. This is a serious food quality issue and completely unacceptable. The only item that was somewhat enjoyable was the fried chicken itself, but that alone could not make up for the overall experience. Unfortunately, this was one of the worst dining experiences I've had. Food quality and freshness need significant improvement.

author: Vern Lng
text: Came in with only mild expectations as I have seen so many Korean BBQ joints come and go (RIP KbabyQ in Norwood which had the same specialties as Bornga) in Adelaide and having tasted the amazing authentic korean bbq IN korea. The expectations were luckily met at a passing standard. Ordered Classic A set, Haemeul Pa Jeon (seafood pancake), Sundubu Jiggae ( spicy tofu seafood soup), extra serve of woo gyeopsal (beef belly) and Daepar Useol ( thinly sliced beef tongue). All up $200++. Meat quality is good here, no bad smells and the marinade for the beef belly is honestly delicious. The sundubu jiggae can be skipped as it was nothing special and tasted like shin ramyeon seasoning added to boiled seafood marinara from coles with some tofu and an egg added in ( no joke). Seafood pancake was thiccc with ingredients and my partner liked it. But was only passable to my tongue after eating the seafood pancake from Park Bong Sook around the corner. Not to my taste I suppose. The reason why I gave the food 3 out of 5 though is because of the quantity. The amount of food that came out with the Classic A set ($98)leaves alot to be desired. Very expensive for what it is, especially for today's economy. I can see why they are no longer fully booked so soon after their grand opening last month. There is not much value here for the specific demographic population that shops and eats in Chinatown. Kosso just below the shop here or even Bewon at Hutt St offers better value in my humble opinion. However full marks on service, cleanliness and atmosphere. All the servers were courteous, respectful, well trained and smiley. And honestly very clean compared to most other restaurants in chinatown (iykyk). If I were to come back, it would be if I were to bring my business clients or my white in laws to experience something different. Long story short, if you dont eat much but like to try kbbq, come. If you are looking to eat alot for good value, then dont come.

text: I went back and forth on whether to order the tangsuyuk because of some of the reviews, but I’m glad I did. Maybe the restaurant has already taken the feedback into account. As you can see in the second picture, the meat-to-batter ratio was well balanced and tasted great. This is a good place to visit with friends since you can share different dishes, but even if you’re dining alone, you can always take the leftovers home. I had a pleasant experience overall. The food was good, the portions were generous, and I left satisfied. Would definitely come back.

author: Anu Kaur
text: Hey Everyone. So us as a couple from NZ went to try this highly rated place for Korean BBQ. We spent 79$, which is not bad as we expected to be paying the double coz we thought it would be gooood. Highly disappointed is what I wanna say. It’s a big place. Staff is not helpful with anything. It’s a self service for the sides, but the sides section was empty and refilling was never done. They told us it’s coming, but we barely had any sides. Meat cooking plates are horrible as they keep burning and they keep changing it after you request them 2-3 times. I wasn’t able to cook the meat well. I have been to many Korean BBQ places in few countries; but this one is just horrible. Garlic was stale, lettuce was in super small pieces so we couldn’t make a wrap style and eat. That’s how normally we have experienced BBQ to be. They don’t explain you anything, we are expected magically to know everything, like how to control the heat of the BBQ as well. I would not recommend it at all. We had such a great day touring for 12 hours and ended up being still hungry and upset after our dinner experience.

author: Biu Biu Su
text: I was extremely disappointed with my experience here, mainly due to the poor communication from the staff at the cashier. I ordered the Korean jjampong noodle set after being informed that it came with 2 dumplings, as stated on the menu. However, when the food was served, I found that the dumplings had been replaced with 2 pcs of mashed potatoes instead! When I asked the staff about the discrepancy, I was simply told, “The kitchen has no stock, so we gave mashed potatoes instead.” No prior notice was given when I placed my order. Had I known the dumplings were unavailable, I would not have ordered the set menu in the first place!! As this is a Korean restaurant, I also asked whether there were any side dishes such as kimchi available. Again, I was met with a brief response: “No stock”! My main concern is not that certain items were unavailable, but rather the lack of communication and transparency. Customers should be informed in advance when key menu items are unavailable or substituted, so they can make an informed decision before ordering!!

author: Monica Chen
text: We went on Mother's Day, and the tableside service was excellent. However, one thing really made everyone at the table want to give us a negative review. The pork selection was already limited, and my dad could only eat pork. We ordered one of the dishes, "Secret Sauce Pork Ribs." It was supposed to be tableside grilled, but suddenly a male staff member interrupted and said, "Our pork ribs have already been grilled in the kitchen, okay?" Without waiting for our "okay," he left in a flurry. First: If you're a tableside service restaurant, and you really want to grill in the kitchen, at least bring the meat over to check and confirm before leaving. Second: It arrived cold and extremely dry. Are you sure it wasn't grilled by mistake for another table and sent over? Even the lady who grilled it for us was shocked! Third: It was incredibly rude, and the pork selection was already limited!! My dad really didn't get to eat anything delicious.

They're all delicious and can be enjoyed with the main dish. 👍 The restaurant is spacious and has a relaxed atmosphere. It was quite empty when I visited, so I was able to enjoy my meal at a leisurely pace. 😊 🌶️ Highly recommended for those who love spicy food 🍲 For those who want to try a delicious and spicy Korean soup! This is a restaurant I would definitely like to visit again when I'm craving Korean food in Makati! ✨

If you are a big eater better off to go to a buffet restaurant. Note the weekend surcharge from this restaurant. Personally I am not a fan of weekend surcharge. I will support restaurant not imposing weekend surcharges. Overall I recommend this restaurant and I will come back with my families.

author: Bella Kim
text: Dined at Bornga yesterday and had mixed feelings about the experience. The meat was excellent, great quality and definitely the highlight of the meal. However, we ordered the Kimchi Seafood Soup, and it was extremely oily. The surface was almost entirely covered in oil, making it difficult to enjoy, so we only ate the seafood and other fillings rather than the broth itself. We have dined here before and have ordered similar soups many times, and we’ve never experienced it being this oily. After waiting quite a while without anyone checking on our table, a staff member finally came over and we raised our concern. The soup was taken back to the kitchen, but when the supervisor returned, we were told that the soup is supposed to be that oily and that nothing could be done because we had already eaten half of it. Unfortunately, what we had actually eaten was mostly the filling, not the broth. While we understand that dishes can vary and not every complaint warrants a replacement, the response lacked the level of hospitality and customer care we expected. It was a disappointing experience, especially given how much we enjoyed the quality of the meat. Great food, but the handling of customer feedback left a lot to be desired.

author: Jan Sy Photo Blogs
text: Bornga Sydney: Hands down the best Korean BBQ grill I've ever tried! This place set the bar so high for Korean BBQ that I don't think anything else is going to come close anytime soon. Let's start with the side: Kimchi, salads, little bites of everything that perfectly balance out the richness of the grill. They're refillable too, so don't be shy! The fresh lettuce and vegetables for wrapping the meat were crisp and clean. Now the beef? absolute standout. Their beef-loin is their signature for a rea son. Paper-thin, beautifully marbled, it crisps up on the charcoal grill like nothing I've had before, fatty, melty, and just perfectly done when wrapped in lettuce with garlic. The Wagyu was next-level too, tender, juicy, that real melt-in-your-mouth quality you'd expect from a top-tier yakiniku spot. The restaurant itself is spacious, clean, bright, and the staff were attentive throughout. Yes, there's a 90-minute window, but honestly? Pace yourself, enjoy every bite, and it's more than enough time to have a full, satisfying Korean BBQ experience. 10/10! I will be back, and I'm already bringing everyone I know. 🔥🥩

I even went to the next restaurant and bought my own containers, yet the staff still refused to help pack the food. What made it worse was seeing them give takeout containers to another customer without any problem. The inconsistency, rude attitude, and lack of basic customer service were extremely disappointing. I definitely would not recommend this place.

author: Chen H.
text: I had lunch with my mom at Benjia Korean BBQ Dunnan branch, and it was a fantastic experience! We had a reservation for 1 pm, but arrived about 30 minutes early. The staff were very friendly and helpful, quickly seating us – a big plus for the service. Upon sitting down, we were greeted by a generous selection of side dishes (and unlimited refills!), each one incredibly flavorful. The vegetable platter was fresh and crisp, beautifully presented, and a perfect complement to the BBQ, cutting through the richness. There was also a bowl of tangy and spicy appetizer soup that instantly whetted our appetites. This time, we ordered the set meal for two, plus an additional $880 to upgrade to SRF premium black beef short ribs, and also added a seafood pancake. The meat was truly excellent – ​​tender, juicy, and with a wonderful charcoal-grilled aroma – you could taste the quality. If I weren't in the later stages of pregnancy with a limited stomach capacity and unable to eat too much oily food, I would have loved to order even more meat! However, the most surprising part of the meal was actually the seafood pancake! It's nothing like your average thick, batter-like omelet; it tastes more like a "deluxe seafood omelet": very eggy, incredibly garlicky, and generously filled with seafood. Crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, it's absolutely delicious and a must-try! There are many other dishes on the menu I'd like to try; I look forward to coming back again!

When it first opened, I heard it was delicious, so I went without reading reviews, thinking I absolutely had to visit when I went to Amsterdam (I probably wouldn't have gone if I had). author: jhonny Corgi
text: I visited this restaurant on May 24, 2026. I ordered pork belly, pork ribs, and kimchi. The food arrived within 7 minutes, which was normal. BUT!!! The side dishes, especially the vegetables and kimchi, took forever to arrive. I had to tell the staff that this was the third time I'd asked for them. To make matters worse, when the staff came to grill the food for me, or even when I asked for more side dishes, it felt like I was begging them. I felt very uncomfortable because they seemed annoyed the whole time. In short, the overall atmosphere was very uncomfortable, making the food taste unpleasant. I ate while feeling very uncomfortable. ***From the time I finished eating until I was about to pay the bill, I only just noticed there were tissues stuck next to my table. I saw them at the next table. There was no notification whatsoever. ****For anyone who enjoys eating in an uncomfortable environment, I recommend avoiding this branch.***

author: Emma_Thailand
text: We ate at Saemaeul's Central Nakhon Pathom branch. The atmosphere was nice, clean, and met high standards. The staff provided good and fast service, constantly checking on the meat grilling process. 🥓✨ The food tasted great, especially their signature spicy pork BBQ. The pork was tender and juicy, with a rich, authentic Korean sauce. Grilled over a fragrant charcoal grill and served with fresh vegetables and side dishes, it was incredibly delicious. The meal cost around 400-600 baht per person, which was great value considering the quantity and quality of the ingredients. 🥩😋 Recommendation: The restaurant is open from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM, following mall hours. It can get quite crowded on weekend evenings, so it's recommended to make a reservation or arrive before peak hours for a more comfortable seating experience. Parking at Central Nakhon Pathom is spacious and easy. For those who love authentic Korean BBQ, this branch is a reliable choice. 🅿️🚗

text: Jajang-myeon = Flavour-packed black bean sauce loaded with chopped onions that pairs perfectly with the thick chewy noodles 🤤 Mapo Tofu Over Rice = Smooth silky tofu, generous minced pork, and an addictive savoury sauce that goes insanely well with rice 🍚 Tang-suyuk = Sauce on the side is the way to go! Crispy lightly-coated pork that stays tender and juicy inside. Dip it into the sweet & sour sauce and it gets even better ✨ ⭐ Really love how they reinterpret classic Chinese dishes with a Korean twist. You can taste the unique flavours while still keeping the essence of the original dishes! Catch more reviews on IG/TikTok @NOMREX

text: Had a really satisfying dinner at Saemaeul Sikdang. The meat quality was great, especially the yeoltan bulgogi which was flavorful, smoky, and cooked perfectly. author: Piraya Mitrsomwang (Chocokasa)
text: I'm usually a big fan of Saemaeun, even a member who pays 5000 won a month just to eat at this restaurant, but this express branch is unacceptable. The first time, the meat was spoiled, but I forgave them, considering it a mistake, and they even offered me steamed egg as an apology. However, the second time, the kimchi was spoiled, and that was it. It wasn't a coincidence. The price isn't that much different from a regular meal; for a little more, you could get shabu-shabu, but this is just a set meal. Shouldn't they maintain the freshness of their ingredients instead of serving old, leftovers to cut costs? The second time, they brought me steamed egg, which I didn't eat because I was afraid of getting a worse stomach upset. I'm done with this restaurant.

author: YJ Yang
text: Yesterday, Saturday, around 7 PM, seven of us visited. It was busy with no empty seats, but fortunately, we were able to get the table we wanted. However, although we ordered various dishes (such as spicy braised chicken and steamed eggs), some took over 20 minutes to arrive, and the staff forgot about them. Anyway, we ended up eating other bulgogi dishes and seafood pancakes. The taste was quite satisfying (although the seasoning was a bit saltier than in Korea, as it was tailored to Thai palates...). They were also very generous with refills of side dishes, which was nice. I plan to visit again with my family next time, perhaps during a less busy time. (It was a bit disappointing, though, as the kitchen staff seemed a bit understaffed.)

author: Siu Po
text: I’ve had a lot of Korean food before, but this was my first time trying Korean-Chinese style dishes, which is why I chose this place. I ordered the black bean noodles, spicy seafood noodle soup, and sweet & spicy chicken. Unfortunately, the black bean noodles were disappointing. The sauce was bland, not sweet, not salty, just like very little flavor. The noodles were slightly hard, and the dish was served cold (I’m not sure if that’s normal). The spicy seafood noodle soup didn’t look very appetizing, but it actually tasted better than the black bean noodles. Still, it lookslike that Im eating instant ramyun at home, nothing particularly special and the filling was also bad, overcooked veggies and a little seafood. The sweet & spicy chicken was okay. Not bad, but not memorable either. Side dishes are self-service and free, but there were only two options: onions and pickled radish. Overall, it wasn’t my taste. Maybe this is how the dishes are supposed to be. If I get the chance to visit Korea, I will try this cuisine again there.

author: Jungwon H
text: We are a Korean family who have lived in Europe for almost two decades, while returning to Korea at least once a year. So we know very well what Korean-Chinese food should taste like, and we also understand what to realistically expect in Europe. Unfortunately, this was by far the worst experience we’ve had. Even our seven-year-old, who was very hungry, refused to eat the food. The jjamppong soup was strangely sour, and the vegetables were completely soggy, as if they had been sitting in the broth for far too long. There was hardly any seafood. The noodles in the jajangmyeon were overcooked and mushy. Given the prices, we expected either better food or better service. The service felt almost self-service, cafeteria-style. If there were an option to give zero stars, I would. That said, the staff were friendly, and the tangsuyuk was slightly better than the other dishes, but overall, I would avoid. As this was not a question of authenticity. We are fully aware that Korean-Chinese food in Europe may differ from what we have in Korea. The real issue was not whether it was perfectly authentic, but whether the food was prepared with professionalism, care, and basic culinary quality. In this case, it simply did not feel that attention or standard was there.

author: Viktoria Türk
text: Dear Viktoria, Thank you so much for your wonderful 5-star review! We truly appreciate your kind words and are very happy to hear that you enjoyed the food, service, and atmosphere. It means a lot to our team to receive such positive feedback. We work hard to maintain the authentic flavors and standards of our South Korean franchise, so knowing that you had a great experience really motivates us. We look forward to welcoming you again soon!

author: Roast Beef
text: EXTREMELY BAD service and food. Came on a Saturday for lunch on 14 Dec, the staff manning the cashier with orange glasses kept frowning and showing bad attitude. From the moment my family and I stepped in until the end she was PISSED?? Told her 5 people and she replied: “HUH!? 5?!” with the most fed up face ever. Ok never mind, maybe she had a bad day because it looked like she was running a one-man show while the other staff was sleeping at a table. (still shouldn’t vent on customers though) Didn’t give us the free brown rice drink at all, only noticed when other people came in at the end of our meal and they had it..what da heck? i guess we had to ask for it. And the radish with onions, we had to ask her at the counter and she rolled her eyes and made her dissatisfaction known by slamming the poor dish around lol. When she served us our food, she gave side eye 😒 frowned and curtly (basically) demanded “who ordered ice cream!?”. My father was like 😐? We thanked her anyway. So the food came, I ordered Jjajangmyeon, stir fried Jjamppong and Fried Soy Garlic chicken. At first the portions looked big, generous with sauce but i only found 1 piece of pork in my Jjajangmyeon 😂 I couldn’t help but laugh, sauce was watery and almost tasteless. The Jjamppong unfortunately had half uncooked sotong and was tasteless too. Chicken was alright but kind of had little sauce drizzled over it. Was looking forward to trying this place but ended up really disappointed. Oh well, at least my parents liked the coconut ice cream. 🤷🏻‍♀️

author: Low Krystabella
text: Dining at Paik’s Noodle @ Suntec City was a real delight. From the moment the bowl of jajang-myeon arrived, you could tell the noodles were handmade with care: a pleasing chew, a fresh springiness that set them apart. The rich black-bean sauce hugged each strand perfectly, delivering that comforting savoury depth one hopes for. The jjamppong was equally impressive — a generous broth bursting with seafood, pork and vegetables, the faint wok hei aroma lingering in the air, teasing the palate before the first slurp. Spicy, bold but balanced. As for their tangsuyuk (sweet-sour pork), it was a standout. The batter was crisp and light, the meat tender, and the sauce brought just the right amount of tang and sweetness without overwhelming the dish. A great choice whether you dip or have the sauce poured over. Combined with the bright, open-kitchen environment and the view over the fountain at Suntec, this is casual dining elevated by thoughtful execution. I’ll happily return — not just for the brand or hype, but because the food truly delivers.

author: TamRoy Mhee
text: I usually eat at the Sukhumvit 39 branch, but today I had the chance to try the Siam Discovery branch. I can say the standards are still as good as ever. The atmosphere here is bright and modern, and it's very convenient to get to, perfect for coming with friends or family. The food tastes like the familiar Bornga style – authentic Korean. They currently have a promotion: buy 2 meat dishes, get 1 free, but since there were only two of us, we couldn't finish it all, so we didn't order it. Dishes we ordered today: Kimchi Jjigae: Rich, sour, spicy, and well-balanced flavor. Steamed Egg: Soft, fluffy, and served hot. Spicy Korean Pork: Tender pork, the sauce is well-absorbed, and the spiciness is just right, not overpowering the sweetness of the pork. My favorite part is the side dishes. They're all delicious and you can refill them as much as you like. They taste great, not just there to have something to offer. We enjoyed the food so much that some items were finished before the main courses! Bornga, no matter the branch, maintains consistent quality. The Siam Discovery branch is a great option – easy to get to, delicious food, and amazing side dishes. If you're a fan of Korean food, you won't be disappointed.

author: Sajith Rangama
text: First time dining here while visiting Bangkok, and I was definitely not disappointed. Bornga Korean Restaurant truly stands out as one of the best Korean restaurants I’ve tried. The food portions are generous, incredibly delicious, and beautifully prepared. The banchan and fresh vegetables are constantly refilled upon request, which makes the experience even better. The restaurant itself is classy and comfortable—one of the nicer places to eat, especially great for large groups. I ordered the dolsot bibimbap and miyeok guk, and everything was cooked to perfection. You can choose to cook it yourself, but the staff prepared mine flawlessly. Located in Siam Discovery, this spot is absolutely worth a visit. Highly recommended!

author: יוספה מרמור
text: This is my first time at a Korean restaurant and it was indeed an experience. We explained to the waitress that we don't eat pork and seafood and they were really attentive and made sure to remove the kimchi made from crabs and a few other things we didn't recognize from the table. It would have been nice if they had the kimchi made from cabbage without the crabs and other sauces that weren't based on seafood, but that's our problem. In addition to the beef we grilled on the fire, we also ordered a beef stew that was delicious but personally a bit spicy for me. The highlight for me was the lotus root that was pickled in a sweet sauce and completely enhanced the flavors of the meat. Personally, I enjoyed the taste of the grilled brisket more and less of the sirloin. A completely different and unique experience. At the end of the meal, we were treated to watermelon.

author: Rojjana Rungrattwatchai
text: BORNGA at Siam Discovery. I like this branch; the view is great, it has a Korean vibe, and you can see the BTS train passing by in the distance. It's a Korean restaurant by famous chef "Baek Jong-won." I had the chance to try four new dishes: soy sauce marinated crab (the crab meat was fresh and served with rice, marinated until the soy sauce had permeated the meat), Haejangguk (a beef stew with tender beef, the soup was rich, spicy, and flavorful), garlic marinated beef tongue (crispy and tender, with a strong garlic aroma when grilled), and pork neck (with thin layers of fat, very tender). Don't forget to order the steamed egg custard and Korean pizza too!

There are always promotions with credit cards. - The restaurant is located on the 2nd floor of Siam Discovery. Parking is available at both Siam Paragon and Siam Discovery. You can use your receipt to get a free parking bus stamp. - Wheelchair accessible.

We also ordered galbitang soup and sundubu jjigae through GrabFood. The galbitang soup is made by simmering beef short ribs for a long time. It was a rich and flavorful soup. It's a bit far to walk from Phrom Phong station, so taking a taxi is recommended.

They have a unique sour seaweed soup that's refreshing. The staff are friendly, cheerful, and lovely. Overall, it's a great restaurant, and I'll definitely come back again.

author: 弘保吉将
text: I made a special reservation in advance and had a set course meal. It was expensive, but even I, a big eater who eats twice as much as the average person, was full. When I asked the chef, I found out he was originally a Michelin star chef overseas. As a food lover, we got along well and chatted for a while. It was no longer just ramen, but a complete set course meal. It's hard to find food of this level even in Japan. The last thing that impressed me was the European-Asian fusion dish that combined the duck with melinjo, which created a completely new flavor. I would recommend this set course to anyone who knows the food. It was the best meal I've had in Thailand in the last 10 years.

author: Laura Kim
text: As a Korean, I was really excited to hear that Bornga Korean BBQ opened in Adelaide, and I had high expectations going in. Pros: 1. Food I really liked the variety of side dishes—a few fresh salad banchan were very much like traditional Korean banchan (except the seaweed). The mu-saengchae (spicy radish salad) was especially outstanding. I also loved that the doenjang jjigae (soybean soup) comes with a salad bowl you can mix and eat together—such a nice touch. The pajeon was thick and crispy, just how it should be. 2. Charcoal BBQ The charcoal BBQ was definitely a highlight—something you don’t easily find in Adelaide—and it tasted great. What impressed me most was how clean and well-ventilated the restaurant was. There was hardly any smoke, and I didn’t have to worry about my clothes smelling like BBQ afterwards, which was a big plus. 3. Staff All the staff were friendly. I don’t know his name, but the Korean manager was very welcoming and had a great sense of humor. The Korean staff were able to guide us in detail—like exactly when to flip the meat and which side dishes go best together—which made the whole experience more enjoyable. Cons: The music was a bit too loud, which made the space feel slightly overwhelming at times. It was a bit pricey. Overall, it was a very enjoyable dining experience, and I’m glad to have a place like this in Adelaide.

author: Summer Song
text: We had a wonderful family dinner here with our baby! What impressed me most was how clean and airy the place was. Usually, you expect to leave a BBQ place smelling like smoke, but there was zero smell on our clothes and no smoke at all …

author: Co Nguyen
text: The staff was very nice and helpful. The restaurant was clean and look beautiful. I'm comparing against Bornga in Haymarket, Sydney. I expect a franchise would standardise the way they do business, so I'm very disappoint with Bornga Adelaide. The food was not as good as Sydney branch. To be honest, I'm heavily disappointed because the restaurant takes the name of "Bornga", which is a famous franchise, and cannot live up to the reputation. To veggy station, no store-made kimchi, NO Perilla leave (a must-have in Korean BBQ), too few options for banchan, the sauce is pre-distributed in small plates which will reduce the sauce quality when exposed to air (should have just put a big bottle of the sauce). I was excited and booked a table the moment I know Bornga has come to Adelaide, but turned out it's no different from any other BBQ place, no different from Kosso right next door. Good quality meat can bought from any good butcher, you can buy the sauce from supermarket. A good restaurant should differentiate themselves from other, do things better than the other. In this case, you just ruin a famous franchise. No wonder why Bornga Australia said Adelaide is not under their management, which means their must be some conflict in how the restaurant is run. This is not true Bornga, just a name copy.

author: Maggie Chan
text: BORNGA Korean BBQ WOW… This is a super delicious and high-quality Korean BBQ lunch set. Today's special $59 Pork Combo BBQ Set lunch served for two people with soft drinks and rice. Fresh ingredients with charcoal grill, authentic Korean flavor, and handmade kimchi—it truly tastes like Korea. 🤩 If they also sold Korean soy sauce crab, it would be perfect. There are also many set meals to choose from, with the high-quality Wagyu beef and beef tongue being particularly recommended.

author: Jazz H
text: BORNGA is a well‑known Korean BBQ restaurant - highly rated for flavour, quality, and authentic Korean BBQ experience… An international Korean restaurant chain founded by Paik Jong-won. It is most famous for its signature dish, Woo Samgyeop, which consists of thinly sliced beef brisket dressed in a special secret sauce! The Bornga menu features authentic Korean dishes created by Executive Chef Jong Won Paik, known for operating popular restaurants in Korea. Some of the MUST TRY!! • Woo Samgyeop: The pioneer of thinly sliced beef brisket with a savory-sweet marinade • Yesan Tteokgalbi: Grilled minced beef rib patties with a sweet soy glaze My fav part is their Unlimited Banchan - All BBQ orders include a complimentary, unlimited spread of side dishes like kimchi, pickled radish, and fresh salads. The meat quality is good, friendly service and restaurant is well maintained.

author: Momoko Oka
text: I highly recommend Bornga Korean BBQ Restaurant! The lunch sets are absolutely delicious, packed with flavor, and a great value. Every visit has been consistently tasty, and the service is always friendly. Perfect spot for anyone craving authentic Korean BBQ!

author: Stan Lee
text: I wasn’t planning on leaving a negative review… but after multiple negative experience at once, I honestly feel like I have to 😔 We went in for a late lunch on Saturday. It wasn’t busy at all. We stood at the entrance waiting for someone to arrange a table for our family… and no one helped us for about 5 minutes ⏳ So we ended up just walking ourselves to an empty table. The main BBQ dishes were okay. not terrible, but definitely not impressive either. Unfortunately, after having the soybean soup and cold noodles, my wife, my child and I all had serious stomach aches 🤢 We had to rush to the public toilets twice straight after eating. That really ruined the whole experience. The side dishes and salads looked dried out, like they’d been sitting there for ages 🥗 We asked for fresh ones from the salad bar. The second batch was a little better, but still not what I’d call fresh. Service was also disappointing. One of the female servers was quite unkind. She stacked two rice bowls on top of each other like a tower and placed them right in front of me 🍚🍚 Then she rushed off after putting the food down roughly, without asking where we’d like anything placed. even though the restaurant wasn’t busy at all. I had to rearrange all the dishes myself. When I tried to move the rice bowl to pass it to my family, it was extremely hot 🔥 I ended up burning my hand. I asked her to help move it since she had gloves on, and also asked if she could place the soup in the middle for sharing. Her response? “You can do it by yourself?” 😳 I was honestly shocked and didn’t even know what to say. At that point, we just wanted to leave as soon as possible because of the poor service. Then came the bill. They charged a 15% surcharge. even though it wasn’t a public holiday 💸 My phone payment initially showed $177, but we were charged $198 in total. I didn’t want to argue, so I just paid the extra $20, even though it didn’t feel right. They really need to clearly update and display the surcharge amount on their app bill to avoid this kind of confusion in the future 📱❗ Overall, very disappointed with both the food and the service. Definitely won’t be coming back again 🚫 ——————————————————————— Update the next day 😔 My child has developed serious diarrhoea overnight and is still struggling with stomach pain. I am very sad. I feel I need to report this to the relevant government department so they can investigate the kitchen hygiene standards 🚨🧼🍽️

author: Nikita Kimberly
text: Bornga Korean BBQ is definitely one of the more popular KBBQ spots in Sydney 🔥🍖 It gets really busy, especially on weekends, so be prepared for a bit of a wait ⏳ But honestly, the food makes it worth it 🤍 The meats are high quality, well-marinated, and full of flavor — everything tasted really good and satisfying! There’s also a nice variety on the menu, so it’s great for sharing and trying different cuts with friends 🥬✨ It is a little on the pricier side compared to other KBBQ places, but you can really taste the difference in quality. The overall experience feels a bit more premium too. A solid choice if you’re craving good Korean BBQ and don’t mind the wait 💫 Definitely recommend going early or outside peak hours!

author: Garry T
text: Extremely disappointing experience. We clearly informed the staff multiple times—both while waiting and before ordering—that we have a sesame allergy. Despite this, visible sesame appeared in multiple dishes and sauces. We only asked for one dish to be remade, but the manager initially had the kitchen remove the sesame from the surface and served it back. We then found many sesame seeds still left, so we had to request a remake again. Only after repeated complaints did they finally remake it properly. Throughout the process, there was no apology—just dismissive and unprofessional behavior. They even tried to deny we had informed them and claimed removing the surface sesame was sufficient. This showed a complete disregard for customer health and safety. Overall, a stressful and unsafe dining experience—avoid if you have food allergies.

author: JOSHUA LEE
text: Owner... I really enjoyed the thick soybean paste stew and beef belly on Sunday... I know you use local blue crabs for the marinated crab, but both of us got stomachaches after eating it. I'm not sure if that was the problem, but the beef belly and the seafood soybean paste stew? I don't think those were the issue... I think you should remove the marinated crab from the menu. They washed the shells thoroughly, leaving nothing inside, and I wonder if they just served it with soy sauce. The crabs were so small that there was absolutely nothing to eat in the legs... The service was worse than other restaurants, and I remember it costing around 300,000 won. I want to reiterate that the beef belly and thick soybean paste stew were delicious, but I am telling you this because I think anyone who eats that marinated crab would 100% never visit again.

author: S L
text: I came here on Sunday, December 21, 2025, around 1:20 PM. The Yangnyeom dwaeji galbi had some raw pork. I had already told the person writing the menu to make sure it was cooked properly. Halfway through, some of it was still raw. I then asked for it to be grilled again. This is pork, and raw pork carries the risk of tapeworms. The restaurant was quiet, not crowded. The waiter apologized (but it was from a different person, not the person writing the menu). Then we had already eaten the raw pork. The assistant manager came over to apologize. We appreciated that, and thanked him. But it still didn't change the fact that we had already eaten the raw pork. We were offered a voucher, but we declined because we wouldn't be returning.

author: Jingoo Kim (부산아니)
text: I had a hard time with food during my trip to Yogyakarta. My wife and I decided to go for some Korean food. After finishing our meal, I saw my wife, and her expression turned into a smile. I thought, "We've made the right choice." It seems like food is more challenging here than in Bali. In this difficult place, I found a restaurant called Bonga, and I was able to return to the hotel, albeit briefly, with a happy feeling. The food was on par with what you'd find in Korea. The side dishes were also delicious. In other places, you have to buy water, but perhaps because it was a Korean restaurant, they even gave us two bottles of water: hot and cold. I hope you all have a great time. (It was dark outside. It would have been even better if the staff had greeted us cheerfully.)

author: Chris Seo (Chestnut)
text: The service is a total mess. If the headquarters in Korea can't manage its franchises, I hope they don't open franchises overseas. The food is terrible, the service is terrible, and I'm worried foreigners might misunderstand and think this is a genuine Korean restaurant. This is a prime example of a franchise that undermines the value of K-food. It also doesn't reflect well on the Baek Jong-won brand image. 1. Why is there a bell? I've rang it two or three times and the waiters never come. 2. They grill the meat, but they don't wait long enough for it to be served, so I ended up eating cold meat. 3. To be honest, the first side dishes were served 20 minutes late. I don't think there's any reason for side dishes to be delayed. Most of the dishes are already cooked and you just need to put them in a bowl, so I don't understand why they're taking so long. 4. The seaweed soup was lukewarm, with no ingredients, as if they were serving leftovers. I thought the taste might be a little different overseas, but I was extremely disappointed with the service. I lived in Jakarta for about seven months and Bali for about a month, and I've been to many Korean restaurants, but this is by far the worst Korean restaurant I've ever visited in Indonesia. Please pay attention to the Korean headquarters. Don't just advertise that you have franchises in many countries.

I ordered a refill of side dishes, but they didn't bring them until the end of the meal, which was annoying. The manager spoke a little Korean, so it wasn't too bad. It's the same taste as a Korean franchise. It's one of the few Korean restaurants I can find in Bandung.

author: 한잔hanjan99
text: The atmosphere is nice, but the food is disappointing. It's a chain restaurant, so the boss is a pro-Indo indie. The service doesn't fit the Korean sensibility. I stopped by on my trip to Bandung. I had a good time. It had a strong Korean vibe. It's not what I expected, but it's what it is. Koreans tend to think of it as a Korean restaurant run by Indonesians. The food isn't that different. However, from the side dishes to the refills, it has a distinctly pro-Indo ambiance. The difficult life of a foreign worker. A restaurant run by locals under the name of a Korean restaurant. I'm grateful, but sometimes it's a bit disappointing. Still, I had a good time. I guess Koreans won't be going there anytime soon. Haha. The packaging is a total mess. ~~ I hope you prosper. ~~~

author: viena purnamasari
text: Bornga Korean Restaurant on Jl. Dr. Rajiman, Bandung (inside the Tama Boutique Hotel building) is a premium Korean restaurant with a comfortable, elegant, and fully air-conditioned atmosphere. Its signature menu is Korean BBQ with quality meats, generous banchan (side dishes), and signature dishes like kimchi jjigae and budae jjigae. The friendly service and clean space make it perfect for family meals, special occasions, or for those who love authentic Korean cuisine. Prices are relatively high (around Rp 200,000 per person), but the quality of the food and atmosphere is commensurate.

At least the soft tofu stew is better. It is a place that looks fancy on the outside but isn't. The food is expensive, too.

text: Compared to the original Jakarta location, both the taste and service were subpar. I ordered bossam, marinated short ribs, naengmyeon (cold noodles), bibimbap, and seafood pajeon (spicy green onion pancake), but they all felt lacking something. The seafood pajeon should probably be called an egg pancake. There wasn't much seafood or green onions, just a lot of egg batter. The marinated short ribs weren't seasoned well either, and the overall flavor seemed to need a bit of a re-examination.

text: This is a franchise of Saemaeul Restaurant in Korea. The taste is similar, but the atmosphere and interior seem much brighter. The staff all seem young and energetic. They greet customers in Korean with "Hello. This is Saemaeul Restaurant" and "Thank you" when leaving, which really makes them feel good. Saemaeul Restaurant's most famous dish is the "7-Minute Pork Kimchi Stew." Pour the kimchi stew over rice and be sure to add some seaweed (gim) before mixing it in. It's delicious.

author: Maro G
text: My visit to Saemaeul Sikdang Mongolia was a huge disappointment. When we arrived, the staff didn’t bring us tea — we only got it after asking. They also didn’t give the warm towel that restaurants usually provide before eating. The menu was confusing, and the ordering system was poorly organized. The kimchi jjigae included in the combo was not even soup — it was almost like a stir-fry. The atmosphere inside felt cold and uncomfortable. There were drunk people shouting across the room, which made it impossible to relax. Overall, the service was terrible — honestly close to zero. I do not recommend going here; don’t waste your time and money.

author: Joseph Kim
text: I went in after seeing the advertisement photo outside the restaurant that said there was a new lunch menu. However, the food came out looking very different from the photo. It was far less than the amount of food for a typical Mongolian. And when I asked about the different prices on the menu, they said it included tax. I think this is probably the first time I’ve seen this method of displaying prices in Mongolia. Whether it’s a restaurant or a clothing store, they all display prices including VAT, but here at Saemaeul Restaurant, the prices are before VAT. I think you might be surprised later if you order just by looking at the menu. I was disappointed because the amount of food in the photo was so different from the actual amount, and I was disappointed because they tried to trick me with tax on the menu prices.

author: Chattarin Amornpitak
text: Let's talk about cleanliness first. I found pork stuck to the pan and even hair in the sauce! Yes, hair!! But because I was hungry and wanted to try it, I asked for a new sauce bowl and continued eating. Cleanliness and Service: 10/10 - Cleanliness: -2 (Once is enough) - Service: 5 (Front-of-house staff provided good service, were cheerful and smiling. The manager was polite. However, the kitchen staff were chatting loudly and some weren't wearing masks.) - Speed ​​of Service: 3 It took quite a long time to prepare the food. Taste: 10/10 (This review is based on my personal taste.) Side Dishes: - Kimchi: 5 - Seaweed Soup: 10 (Unfortunately, it was served warm and cooled down quite quickly.) But it was still delicious.) - Red sauce: 7 (Not gochujang) - White sauce: 8 (Mayo garlic) - Rice: 7 - Sausage: 3 - Probably pickled radish (purple): 7 Main dish: - Original pork belly: 5 Pros: - Waitstaff and manager were polite and cheerful. - Soup was delicious, sauces were good. Cons: - Cleanliness - Food was slow to come out. P.S. I didn't take pictures of the food, but the manager was aware of it.

author: francois cew
text: Saemaeul A new Korean restaurant in the Gading Serpong area. The place is spacious, clean, and comfortable. There's a wide variety of food. Free banchan and drink refills. Free seaweed soup with beef broth. The meat is fresh. You can grill it at your table or have the staff help you grill it. Free yogurts and ice cream too. Free orange juice for Google reviews. Must-orders: Spicy Yeoltan Bulgogi, Sundubu Jjigae, Naembi Tteobokki All the food here is super delicious and truly comforting. The service is also fast and friendly. I'll definitely be back again 👍👍👍

author: Rebecca Pang
text: Clean , simple and spacious. Unlike some Korean restaurants that tries to maximise space and table arrangements that are close to one another, this restaurant gives you ample of space and feels comfortable. We ordered cold noodles, original pork neck and the premium beef . Would recommend to try the premium beef original!! Super yummy paired with the salt provided ❤️ the texture was just nice as the meat wasnt too tough marbled with little fats~ for me the pork neck is average ~but the cold noodle was really refreshing and appetising ❤️ my husband says will definitely be back for the beef and cold noodle & looking forward to try other dish as well

author: Mina
text: My husband and I ordered the Yeoltan Bulgogi for two, one 7-Minute Kimchi Stew (including rice), and one bowl of plain rice, and the bill came to just over 1,000 baht. They added a 7% and 10% service charge, haha. The side dishes generally tasted like authentic Korean food, and eating perilla leaves for the first time in a while gave a fresh feeling that reset my stomach after feeling a bit queasy. The 7-Minute Kimchi Stew was slightly lacking in flavor, but the Yeoltan Bulgogi tasted almost exactly like the ones in Korea. Also, side dishes are refillable, so feel free to order more without hesitation. One other disappointing thing was that the staff were all completely devoid of energy. While we were looking at the menu before ordering, they kept staring at us with their hands on their hips, which was quite uncomfortable... Also, the person grilling the meat and the server all had expressionless faces. They must be having a hard time. If you miss the taste of Korea, give it a visit, but don't expect too much.

author: Suhee Jung
text: During the day, the staff won't let you sit near the window because it's not open. Even in the late afternoon, when there aren't many customers, they can't serve you all the way to the window, so they only leave the entrance area open. If the owner sees this, I hope he'll consider making changes from the customer's perspective. There's a Saemaeul restaurant in Central World, but the one in Won Bangkok is the one with the open view and the window seats on Rama 4 Road. Thais are the same. They keep the window seats closed, day or night, and force customers to the entrance area... I hope they'll consider this from the customer's perspective.

text: We arrived around 1:00 PM and the tables were full, but we waited no more than 15 minutes. We were seated at a window-side table with a great view. The restaurant uses high-quality meats, and the staff grills them right at your table. The various cuts of pork were grilled to perfection. The sauce was rich but not overpowering. Other dishes, like the kimchi soup and the steamed cheese egg, were also delicious.

We had a combo which mixed three types of meat perfectly with good sauce and generous side dish. Staff are so much friendly and helpful, from at table serving to billing. They always make sure we are good with their offerings. Recommended for a relaxed dinner with colleagues after a hectic work day.

author: D3AR C
text: Passed this popular restaurant a few times and decided to visit early one evening since there wasn't a queue. Being a huge fan of Chef Paik, this has to be good! While the variety is not huge, mainly pork, they do have some cuts of beef available. Banchans are refillable and pretty good. The kimchi jigae is good just small in terms of portion. Corn cheese is ok but the meats are truly the stars. Our server K.Nan grilled the meats to perfection and was very attentive too. Service is good but come early else you'll have to queue. The only downside is that you'll walk out smelling of the grilled meats, unlike other BBQ restaurants. And the bench seatings are a little tight; tables are low so watch out for your knees to prevent unnecessary bruising! Alcohol selections are ample. Overall, a satisfying authentic meal .

text: Korean style barbecue in Central Ladprao. It's a set of pork and beef marinated in various sauces with many side dishes that you can refill as you please. author: Mist _in_Blue
text: The restaurant is on the 7th floor, near Haidilao. I've eaten here in Korea, but this is my first time trying it in Thailand. It's just as delicious. Yeoltan Bulgogi (฿290) - The staff will stir-fry the pork for you. It's served with sauce and sesame leaves, which is a perfect match. There are a ton of side dishes, including kimchi, pickled vegetables, and smooth mashed potatoes. It's a fun and satisfying way to cut through the richness. You can request extra for all the side dishes. Japchae (฿320) - The noodles are soft and incredibly delicious. Chilbun Dwaeji Kimchi (฿280) - The kimchi soup is perfectly sour. Prices do not include a 10% service charge and 7% VAT. There's a 50฿ discount on Samsung Galaxy devices. Payment is also possible via TrueMoney.

text: I had high expectations. From the moment I entered the restaurant, I gestured with my fingers to the staff, who just stared at me, asking how many people were in my party. They even asked again how many. When we got to the table, they grilled the pork for us, creating a huge cloud of smoke that left us with a lingering smell. Once it was finished grilling, the table was full of plates. The staff kept pushing the plates to make room for serving, pushing them so hard that they almost fell onto our laps. There was no apology whatsoever. I really wish they would train their staff better. The price I paid didn't justify the service I received.

author: Paolo Noro
text: At Mega Bangna, there's a Korean restaurant well worth a visit. The atmosphere is modern yet warm, making you feel instantly at ease, with spacious tables and a friendly staff. Dishes arrive quickly and are served with great care: the grilled meat is tender and flavorful, and the selection of side dishes (the famous banchan) is rich and varied, with generous portions to share. Even traditional dishes like bibimbap and kimchi jjigae maintain an authentic flavor, without being overly touristy. An ideal place for both a dinner with friends—thanks to the barbecue tables that create a convivial atmosphere—and a quick lunch after shopping. Prices are average for Bangkok, but more than justified by the quality and overall experience.

author: huyền nguyễn
text: This restaurant is very special to me, as it holds many memories from the time I lived in Bangkok. It was always our go-to place whenever we craved barbecue. My boyfriend is Korean, and he also really loves the food here. The staff are very attentive – they help grill the meat for you and always notice when the banchan is running low, bringing refills for free. The meat is tender, perfectly grilled, and the seasoning has a unique, delicious flavor that I haven’t found at other Korean restaurants in Bangkok. What impressed me the most, though, is the warmth and helpfulness of both the staff and the managers. Thank you so much 🙏

In Korea, there are occasionally places that replace the charcoal grills with gas grills, but for the first time in a while, I enjoyed authentic charcoal-grilled bulgogi to my heart's content. Highly recommended.

The service was disappointing. We won't be returning and wouldn't recommend it. If there are problems, the staff tend to ignore them.

author: Kosmas PT
text: This Korean restaurant is delicious, from the side dishes to the grilled meat. I highly recommend it. It's a made-to-order system where you choose your preferred cut of meat, and the staff will grill it for you. Side dishes are free and unlimited refills. I recommend the unmarinated meat; it's delicious with the side dishes. The staff provides excellent service, changing the grill frequently, refilling side dishes constantly, and politely asking about customer needs. Highly recommend this restaurant; I will definitely be back.

The Sundubu soup was very bland and tasteless. I wouldn't recommend it and won't be going back.

author: Orrahappy
text: Semaeun at The Charn Chaengwattana is a very nice Korean BBQ spot with excellent service. The staff are friendly, attentive, and quick to assist. The food quality is solid, and both the pork and beef sets are tasty and definitely worth the price. Side dishes are unlimited and can be topped up at any time, making the meal even more enjoyable. The steamed egg with cheese is also amazing—soft, fluffy, and perfectly balanced with the melted cheese. It’s a must-try! Overall, this is a great Korean BBQ restaurant with good value, delicious food, and excellent service. I’d happily come back again! ⸻ Semaeun The Charn Chaengwattana Semaeun at The Charn Chaengwattana is a very good Korean BBQ restaurant. The service is excellent. The staff are attentive and helpful. The food is of high quality. Both the pork and beef sets are very good value. The side dishes are refillable, making the meal even more enjoyable and delicious. The steamed egg with cheese is also very delicious. The egg is soft and fluffy, and the cheese is a perfect combination. It's a must-try! Overall, this is a delicious barbecue restaurant with great value and impressive service. I'll definitely be back!

author: John Oden
text: We ordered the large set for 4 people all the meat came with the same sauce, quality of the meats were ok but all were covered up by the sauce. Service was very slow, for the refills of the vegetables, kitchen and garlic. Everything that was unlimited refills would take so long to be replenish, you had to asked for it many times. We came at 1:30 pm with only 3 tables including ours. I also wanted to order some food for take home and the staff said what I ordered was only for the food delivery, so I ordered for self pick-up with the waitress help and paid by the app (GRAB) at 2: 15 pm by 2:31 pm the app told me to go pick-up my food as it was ready. The waitress told me to go to front counter to pick up the order, guy at the counter had no idea what I was talking about, I showed him my phone and the order. He told me to wait at my table, 5 minutes later he came to me and asked what I ordered. 3 pm asked again and he went to the kitchen and asked where is this order, I'm standing right next to him when the kitchen staff said no order no information. Manager came and said the staff never had self pickup order before and I would have to wait another 30 minutes or I can cancel my order though the app. I said no and demanded for my money back, Later came and transferred money into my account and left.
