author: Yuiiz Parudee
text: I've never tried the express service before, so I decided to give it a try. I wasn't very impressed. The side dishes were incomplete, and the pork slices looked much better and more appetizing than at regular restaurants. The portion size was also significantly smaller, but I understand they're trying to lower the price. I had to ask for things multiple times, and requested a change of grill about four times before they finally came. I was getting really frustrated. I also had to ask for the side dishes several times. There weren't even that many customers, but I was annoyed by the constant calling. One time is enough. I'll go back to the regular service; it's tastier and more convenient.

text: Saemaeul, a famous Korean BBQ restaurant, has a service-based system at this branch, unlike the self-service Saemaeul Express branch. Upon entering, staff will recommend the menu. I ordered the pork set, which came with 7-minute kimchi soup and japchae (stir-fried glass noodles with fish cake). The pork was excellent, with minimal gamey taste and very tender. Importantly, I liked that the side dishes could be refilled as much as you wanted. Anyone who likes grilled pork and eating sesame leaves or sour kimchi shouldn't miss this place.

It's not a buffet, you just pay for what you eat. The taste is good, the marinated pork is delicious, the vegetables are fresh, and the soup is always refillable.

Enjoy a comfortable meal without having to work yourself. - A wide variety of menu items and ingredients.

author: attpocari
text: This famous Korean BBQ restaurant offers a self-service grilling experience with unlimited side dishes, delicious soup, and fresh lettuce for wrapping. They start by serving 3-4 types of dipping sauces plus salt. Everything is excellent; it all depends on your preference. For example, if you like salt, adding kimchi on top creates a great tangy flavor. After that, they serve unlimited delicious seaweed soup. Then, a staff member will come to your table to ask about your order – pork, beef, etc. Just let them know what you'd like first. They'll grill it slowly to your desired doneness. Today, we ordered both pork and beef: Yeoltan Bulgogi (Spicy), Moksal, and Chadolbakee. Personally, I wrap everything in lettuce, add the dipping sauce and side dishes, and eat it all at once in one bite – it's amazing! Another recommended dish is Chilbun Dwaeji Kimchi, served with rice and seaweed. Mix everything together – it's delicious! Overall, the service is great, the food is served quickly, it's delicious, the vegetables are fresh, and the side dishes and soup are unlimited. Honestly, just eating the free side dishes is worth it!

author: The Moonlight
text: First time experiencing SAEMAEUL restaurant. From beginning to end the service was top notch. As soon as I Walked in they knew how many people were in my lanch time and we were led to fairly nice private corner of the restaurant. The staff (FongBeer) included the Hostess were lovely always catering to us and anticipating our needs. The food itself was very delicious. Tons of bold flavors that all seemed to blend together perfectly. Their signature Korean Overall it was a lovely experience and I truly enjoyed my time there. The food was delicious and the service impeccable. If they work on creating more memorable (Lovely Very Good jop ^_^)

author: Gulwariya Siribawonkunjira (IRA)
text: 📍Saemaeul Ratchaphruek Branch Delicious and complete Korean BBQ experience. No need to fly far 🇰🇷🔥 Today, Ira ate with 3 friends as usual. The menu we ordered was DIY rice balls, pork belly, and sliced ​​pork neck – a full Korean BBQ experience. The most impressive thing was: 👉 Unlimited refills of side dishes! So satisfying, we could eat without having to wait for the meat. 👉 The Jaew dipping sauce here is the best! It goes perfectly with the grilled pork – fragrant and spicy. It made the meal even better. Juicy grilled meat, fun to mix your own rice balls. The atmosphere is also perfect for hanging out with friends. Delicious, great value, and we always leave feeling full and satisfied! 😍 For anyone who loves Korean BBQ, I highly recommend trying it! 🌶️🥓🔥

author: Thanon Rujisert
text: I usually go to Korea very often, and I like to stay in Hongdae, which is very close to Saemaeul restaurant, so I eat there all the time. Today, I decided to try it in Thailand. First of all, it was a weekday, so the restaurant was very quiet. Secondly, regarding the taste, I think some things weren't quite the same as in Korea, but some were great. Here are some dishes I recommend: -First, I recommend the Yeoltan Bulgogi. It's considered their signature dish. It's delicious and tastes just like in Korea, but the serving dish looked a bit too new and clean. Maybe I'm not used to it because it's not like in Korea, haha. -Next, another signature dish is the Shinbun Tteokbokki, or what they commonly call "7-Minute Kimchi Soup." This was also excellent, just like the original, even the serving dish. The taste was really good. -Next is the Chadolbagi, which is fatty brisket eaten with salt and pepper. It's delicious. -Next is the pork neck. This is very tasty when grilled hot. It has a nice crunchy texture. -The last thing I ate today was the cheese steamed egg. Hot steamed egg topped with mozzarella cheese. It was delicious. P.S. Side dishes are still refillable as usual. Just ask the staff, because there's no self-serve buffet like in Korea. P.S.2 The staff are very lovely, energetic, and attentive. I'll definitely visit again whenever I have the chance.

author: Ng Wilson
text: The service is okay and the place is clean. But the food is absolutely atrocious in terms of taste. Jjampong - as all stores have their own recipe for Jjampong, it is up to them to decide what is it they want to make it special. But for this case - there is nothing. Mussels and sotong are tiny. No smokey flavor profile. Soup base is extremely boring/bland. Granted its a casual dining place but a little bit of standards will go a long way. Noodle is suspiciously like the yellow hokkien noodle as it has a rather alkaline taste and the texture is too soft to be considered proper Jjampong noodles. Lack of bounciness to the noodle. Overall just a disappointment. Mapo tofu - this is NOT Mapo Tofu. The amount of ingredients are as follow (from my observation so probably non scientific): onions (60%), Tofu (35%), meat (5%). The amount of onion makes me question if the cook understands what is mapo tofu. It tastes super oniony and no numbness nor spiciness to the dish. It should be renamed as onion tofu. Takeout the meat and you probably get a good vegan item on the menu. This is genuinely the worst variant of mapo tofu I ever had.

author: jieun lee
text: A few months ago, I visited the restaurant and the food was delicious, so I came back with my family today. When I arrived at the restaurant around 4 p.m., the employee told me it was closed. My baby and I sat in the food court across the street, intending to wait, and I asked my husband to ask again for the opening hours. The employee, with a somewhat rude expression, simply replied, "An hour." From there, the experience became extremely unpleasant. The employee, perhaps annoyed by our question, glared at me and my husband, who were sitting across from us, for a few seconds after we returned. The bespectacled employee probably remembers us, because it's no exaggeration to say the glaring gaze lasted almost a minute. Why the glaring? We didn't ask angrily. We just smiled and asked politely. The employee's rude attitude made for a very unpleasant experience, and I'm leaving this review in the hope that things will improve on our next visit.

author: 戴小戴
text: On a friend's recommendation, I visited a Korean restaurant 🇰🇷, the restaurant of the famous and popular Korean chef Baek Jong-won 😂. So far, I've tried a few dishes that were pretty good~ Jajangmyeon - $180 The dark black jajangmyeon had a perfect balance of sweet and salty, and the seasoning itself was very mild. It wasn't as sweet as I expected, and it wasn't overly salty either, with a wonderful aroma 👍. The noodles were very chewy and became very smooth after being mixed with the special jajangmyeon. The side dish of pickled radish provided by the restaurant helped to cut through the richness~ Overall, a pretty good combination 👌 Seafood Stir-fried Noodles - $210 Although it looks very red, I personally don't think it's spicy 😂 The broth is only mildly spicy, with a rich flavor that's just right~ The noodles are topped with lots of seafood and vegetables~ and of course, Korean kimchi~ The seasoning is slightly stronger than jajangmyeon (black bean noodles), so if you like spicy food, you can try it spicier~ 👍 Sweet and Sour Pork - $350 The outer layer isn't the typical reddish-orange color found in Taiwan, but rather a white wrapper. It's crispy on the outside and the meat is firm~ The dipping sauce provided is 6/10 in sourness. The sweetness level 4 was perfectly balanced – not too sour at all! 👌 It wasn't greasy at all, which is a nice difference from the typical Taiwanese sweet and sour pork. 👍 Overall, the dining experience was pretty good. 🤣 Neihu is usually quite crowded during lunchtime, with many people dining in. For office workers, the prices aren't cheap. However, it's a good way to treat yourself after a hard day's work or to have a meal with colleagues and friends. While the noise level inside was a bit high, it was still acceptable. I recommend dining with 1-4 people.

author: MMC
text: This was my first time dining at this restaurant. I ordered by scanning a QR code, and throughout the entire process, I didn't see a place to enter the unified code (or similar identifier). When the food arrived, the restaurant simply gave me the invoice. I asked if I could enter the unified code, as I hadn't seen it when paying online… They replied that there was a place to enter the unified code when ordering. Even if I hadn't noticed, they could have stamped the invoice or reprinted it. But they showed no intention of helping me. When I asked them to reprint it, they said, "If you want to cancel the whole order and reorder, then stamp the invoice." Did they expect me to point it out? They seemed to think it was too much trouble and didn't want to deal with it. I then said that I might have missed the place to enter the unified code, even though they knew I wanted it, they just pretended not to understand. The food was also mediocre. The whole restaurant operates like a fast-food chain, but the prices aren't cheap. Two of us ordered sweet and sour pork and hot and sour pork, both small plates, a noodle dish, and a rice dish, totaling $1160 – very poor value for money.

author: HSIN MIN LO
text: The jajangmyeon (black bean noodles) tasted very authentic, just like the ones I had in Seoul before. The other dishes were pretty ordinary. There were very few people on a weekend evening, and plenty of empty seats. Personally, I thought the location was very inconvenient; it's quite far!

author: coco chia
text: This is a one-off Korean restaurant 🏬😍 I ate there in Korea 🇰🇷 before and didn't remember much. Because of the connection between the chef 🧑‍🍳 Baek Jong-won, I opened one in Taiwan and decided to try it again. I can only say don't have high expectations. For the jajangmyeon (black bean noodles), I recommend ordering the original and spicy versions together. The original is ridiculously ordinary on its own, and the spicy is ridiculously spicy, but together they're just right! The sweet and sour pork was so sour it made me cough a lot 😂 I'll eat it if it's edible, but I wonder how long it will stay open? Decor and dining environment: 100/30. Food: 30/30.

author: Brian Leeuw
text: Just passing by. It's probably an experience you only need to try once. The only redeeming quality was that the staff proactively provided an apron. But...but we both ordered seafood noodle soup/rice! Why were they only willing to provide an apron?

author: Grace Chien
text: Single Set Meal NT$2,390 Stir-fried Noodles (Soup) + Stuffed Fried Chicken + 330ml Coke …

author: jiu4shi4chi1
text: Seafood Stir-fry Chili Seafood Stir-fry Fried Rice with Soybean Paste Sweet and Sour Pork

author: Henny Cheng
text: I ordered food from a delivery platform; the sweet and sour pork was delicious, but the noodles with soybean paste were just average.

author: 張家蕙
text: The Zhajiangmian (noodles with soybean paste) was amazing! 😎 …

author: Jojo Liao
text: Having tried Japanese food before, eating Taiwanese food was simply terrible; the taste was vastly different. The jajangmyeon (noodles with soybean paste) was lukewarm, the portion was much smaller, and the amount of soybean paste was extremely limited. The flavors of the chili and soybean paste were also very unbalanced. The seafood jajangmyeon I ordered as an extra treat was also mediocre and had a burnt taste. The sweet and sour pork was freshly fried and delicious! The pickled radish side dish was also good! But it's not worth making a special trip to eat here. And Mr. Bai, have you even tried this yourself? 🤨

author: Lin魯豬
text: I ordered takeout: noodles with soybean paste, stir-fried noodles with toppings, and fried chicken. The fried chicken was so delicious, and the portion was huge! The pickled radish was just okay; it wasn't very flavorful.

author: Yi ling Yeh
text: Just like the jajangmyeon noodles I had in Busan. The flavor of the jajangmyeon noodles and the sweet and sour pork was exactly what I remembered. Delicious! I'd go back again.

author: Carolina Zeng
text: Dined on November 3, 2025. The Zhajiangmian (noodles with soybean paste) was delicious. The stir-fried noodles and sweet and sour pork were average. The oil-drenched chicken was very crispy. I want to try the stir-fried noodles next time!

author: Yuna Lin
text: The zha jiang mian (noodles with minced meat sauce) was warm, with a sweet sauce, and not too salty, making it easy to eat. The fried ma mian (noodles with tomato flavor) had just the right amount of spiciness, so even those who can't handle spice could enjoy it. The sweet and sour pork was chewy, the sauce was just right, and it tasted great with onions. The service was excellent, and there was a self-service recycling system.

author: kzya
text: The jajangmyeon were chewy and flavorful, making every bite satisfying, comforting and absolutely delicious. Thought the tteokbokki weren’t spicy, it was tasty 😋

author: Randy Fadlila Nasser
text: If you’re a fan of Culinary Class Wars on Netflix, you’ll immediately recognize this chef. Come for the big portions and amazing value—I tried the Jjamppong for just $8! A must-visit for Korean food lovers. It's even more fun when you share the dishes with friends or colleagues.

author: Agnes Mei
text: The food is delicious, maybe the best korean food in shv

author: Fintech Travel
text: Sihanoukville Chinese restaurant...the best

author: Hardy Tom
text: The owner is so kind and even though it was my first time there, he provided great service and the atmosphere of the store was great.

author: 해외살이
text: It's been a while since I've had such delicious Chinese food. I highly recommend the Chili Jjajang, especially to those who like spicy food!

author: 파슬리
text: Since it's a newly remodeled place, the store is clean and all the tables and sofas are new, so it smells a little like new furniture. But not enough to be bothersome while eating~ I had the jjambbong, jjajang, and tangsuyuk small size, and the price was really reasonable, and it was so delicious with the aroma of fire! The two owners were so kind and helpful, so I was very satisfied with my meal~ Both the jjambbong and tangsuyuk had a lot of toppings, a lot of meat, and the noodles were chewy. The tangsuyuk was chewy with no gamey smell, so I couldn't stop eating it ㅋㅋ It was really delicious. By the way, dogs are not allowed! I hope it gets delivered soon~~

author: Jono Toohey
text: Delicious and affordable!

author: Lucy Nguyên
text: The food is quite delicious and easy to eat 😌 The menu is diverse, especially the black bean noodles and fried dishes which are pretty good. The space is clean, and the restaurant is easy to find, so it's very convenient if you want to stop by for a quick meal with friends.

author: Jun Kim
text: It is delicious. It tastes a bit better than the Jajangmyeon place in Sensok.

author: Alex higgins
text: Delicious Korean meals here. Would come back again

author: Niu Nathan

author: Lizzy Kim
text: It tastes the same as Hong Kong Banjeom in Korea. The price is a bit higher.

author: La Lune
text: - i order mandu, black bean noodle and sweet & sour pork ( all taste very good) - price very acceptable. My bill came out around 22$ The food took around 10min after we order so its pretty fast.

author: 김선택
text: I like that the restaurant interior is cool and hygienic.

author: Sang
text: Jjajangmyeon and jjambbong are delicious. The cheapest Chinese restaurant in Phnom Penh.

author: Theoun Sreyleak
text: Best jajangmyeon in town but it’s hard to park motobike.

author: JongHa
text: A Korean-Chinese restaurant located in Phnom Penh, Cambodia. So, it's a restaurant that sells jajangmyeon, jjambbong, and tangsuyuk. Think of Baek Jong-won's Hong Kong restaurant. You can get delivery or go and eat there. The staff are friendly and the food is clean and delicious. The location is near the Novotel, a hotel I personally recommend. In addition to the Novotel, I recommend two Marriott hotels. The worst hotel in Cambodia is the Naga Casino Hotel. The hotel is old, the casino staff, and the money changers are really unfriendly, and the casino has a 0 win rate. Never go to the Naga Casino Hotel.

author: にゃごぼん
text: Update: March 2026 For the first month or two of this year, I noticed some slight changes in the noodles, toppings, and soup, but they seem to have returned to normal in March. Their quick response demonstrates excellent operations. -- While the restaurant has the feel of a chain or family restaurant, the noodle dishes are of high quality. Korean-style jjajangmyeon and jjampon are their main dishes, and the sauces and soups are rich in the flavor of the ingredients, while the noodles are chewy and not soggy. Although a bit pricey, the fried dumplings served as a side dish have excellent texture and flavor.

author: Ben BB
text: The lunch is delicious, but the dinner is terrible. The mapo tofu served at lunch and dinner is different. The staff are generally rude. That's the kind of place it is.

author: Anita M
text: Got the jjajamyeon and small size of tangsuyuk. Portion size is huge and both were good. This chain restaurant is consistent with quality and taste of food.

author: SangMok Jeong
text: I recommend Jjajang

author: Peter Bae
text: Clean, good, not the best tasting. And the price is ridiculous.

author: 이춘득
text: During my stay in Phnom Penh, I visited here twice. I tried four dishes: jajangmyeon (black bean noodles), jjambbong (spicy seafood noodle soup), tangsuyuk (sweet and sour pork), and fried rice. Except for the fried rice, everything was …

author: Jacob W
text: The ventilation here is terrible. You end up smelling just like the kitchen staff after your meal! I want to taste the food, not smell like the food! FIX YOUR VENTILATION!!!

author: 呀呀

text: Among dish we ordered, only Jajangmyeon (black bean sauce noodle) I would recommend and I, myself would go back to repeat order. For Tangsuyuk (Fried pork with sweet and sour sauce) I mean it’s my first time trying it, the tastes same as the name! it really sweet, there’s no hint of sour taste but if you’re first time there you can order it and recommend to dip with the black salty sauce in the plate with other side dish and I’m kind of disappointed with the portion of the Ttuek Bokki! It’s way too little than I’m expected. We spent about 8$ per person for 2 bowl of Jajangmyeon and each of other dish which in total 5 plates.

author: Lin Z
text: I ordered Jajangmeyon and Tangsuyuk for take out. The Jajangmeyon got a bit soggy when I get home but it's normal consider it's a bit of a drive. I like the Jajangmeyon but I prefer it's not so sweet. Even the Tangsuyuk is very sweet. I've been complaining to the waitress but they always say it's premade. The price is slightly higher than the rest of the fast-food type restaurant around BKK, but the portion is also relatively bigger. The service is good and the waitress are polite. Overall 7.5 out of 10

author: 코알라
text: It tastes almost exactly like Korean food. It was fascinating to see how they made such tender sweet and sour pork using local pork, and while the stone pot jajangmyeon was a bit sweet, it was good. I think this is top-tier for Phnom Penh. They only do delivery via Grab, though.

author: Sokleap Kry

author: Jay Lee
text: A place I sometimes stop by when I'm home sick. There are more Cambodians than Koreans here. The penetration of K-culture is incredible.

text: I'll come back to try their other dishes.

author: SJ KIM
text: It tastes similar to Chinese food in Korea.

author: A
text: The jjajangmyeon used to be really good last year, but unfortunately it seems to have lost its touch. The noodles they use now are different, and the overall dish just doesn’t taste the same anymore. That said, their sweet and sour pork is still one of the best and remains the highlight for me. I also tried one of their new menu items — the fried chicken — but didn’t realise it was bone-in, as there was no indication on the menu whether it was bone-in or boneless. I think this should definitely be made clearer. Unfortunately, I didn’t enjoy the chicken. There were small bone fragments throughout, and some pieces seemed shattered from the frying process, making it quite unpleasant and honestly a bit risky to eat. Several times I bit into hard bone pieces unexpectedly, to the point where it felt like I could chip a tooth if I wasn’t careful. Also, staff need to be more attentive as we couldn't find anyone to seat us and it took a while for the chicken to come out. It was too long to the point we were going to cancel the order. No side dishes provided except 3 little tiny pickled radish.

author: Olivia Huang
text: It was delicious and reasonably priced. I didn't get to eat it in Korea, but I got to eat it in Melbourne! 😂 The waitresses on the second floor were also very friendly! 👍👍 …

author: JS Travels
text: Fantastic food and was served so quickly! I loved the music there, it was very fun!

author: Dwayne Chamings
text: Total crap food. Dry, no taste. Was there service, had to order on an app at the table.

author: James AU
text: If you’re looking for authentic, flavor-packed Korean-Chinese comfort food, this is the spot. We had an incredible spread, and every dish hit the mark. ### **The Food Highlights** …

author: Hiromi Goodwin

author: Joey
text: The portions were generous, and every dish was packed with rich, satisfying flavour. The jjampong soup stood out with its perfectly deep and comforting broth. For the quality and serving size, the prices are excellent—making this a reliable, wallet-friendly spot I’d happily return to.

author: Hưng Huỳnh
text: Good food, good time hâhha

author: Anna Ha

author: Jilong Zhang
text: Wow for $13.50, the jajangmyeon is really generous, with thick noodles and even thicker sauce. It really hits the sweet spot and I engulfed it quickly.

author: J
text: Pre quick and decent jjajangmyeon. They have their off days but rarely. My go to spot in the cbd as an AUS/korean

author: Lee
text: They use noodles similar to kalguksu, which are different from those in Korean Chinese restaurants, but that is fine. However, they only give you one piece of onion at the start, so you have to ask for more... haha. If you ask for more, they give you a generous amount. The Jjamppong doesn't have that spicy kick; it tastes more like Kimchi Jjamppong. The Jajangmyeon was actually pretty good, and the Tangsuyuk was decent too. Well, considering it's overseas, this is about 70% of the authentic Korean taste, so I do eat it.

author: Fairy Dust
text: Place was clean, service quick. Jjajangmyeon was tasty, seasoned just right. The tteokbokki was a bit too salty.

author: Charmaine Chiam
text: Worse meal I ever had. The snow chicken was boned and cut to pieces causing the pieces to have blunt bone ends. Theres many more Korean places to go around the area

author: Hannah Meryl
text: Food is really good with very valuable price. My friend and I had dinner there and we ordered two Korean chicken dishes, Jajangmyeon and a bottle of Soju for only 62aud. Their side dishes isn't as much. We were given 3 cuts of radish and a peel of onion (i think.. not sure?). Kimchi was an additional cost. Overall, we'll be back! We'd love to try their lunch specials.

author: Ban Y
text: JJampong and Jjajangmyeon sauce are okay but the noodle is very thick and does not match with the sauce at all. If you improve to get the noodle more thiner will be okay. Thick noodle ruins all the taste. And broken easily, its not Korean …

author: NGUYỄN DUY
text: They have meals and bowl taste so delicious however In busy time you may wait a little bit longer, service is alright, i donned indoor so it's hot and stuffy

author: Jono K
text: Had lunch here because my partner and I wanted to eat JJM. Ordering qas quick and the place has two levels. Approx 20 pax bottom level and about 30 upstairs. The sauce for the JJJ was good but the noodles were exceptional and we also had a side of tangsuyuk. In my preference Ive definitely had better else where but pricing was reasonable and serving generous. Menu options are minimal but they seem to be all of a decent standard. Definitely give this place a try if you're in the city.

author: Joe McCauley
text: Good vibe

author: Jade Pham
text: Great food and service

author: Jen I
text: I ordered delivery, and there was a hair in the noodles of the Jajangmyeon. Also, both the Jajangmyeon and the Bokkeum Jjamppong had noodles that were unusually thick, almost like udon, and didn’t taste good. It didn’t seem like they were overcooked or soggy — it felt more like the wrong type of noodles was used. As for the Tangsuyuk, the batter was grey in color, which made me question the quality or condition of the oil used for frying.

author: Long Pham
text: Great place to enjoy dinner with family and friends. Love the vibes and friendly staff

author: Elly K
text: It was my first time trying the soy-flavored briquette bulgogi, and it was delicious!! I asked for spicy sauce to dip it in, and it was so good!! The 7-minute pork kimchi stew is always tasty too; make sure to try them together!

author: Vincent Bowles
text: I only ever ordered the beef rice here, but it’s delicious and the staff are so friendly it’s untrue. The music selection is also very good and the prices are very reasonable. I highly recommend.

text: Very good wait staff. However, the fried chicken was a bunch of bones with barely any meat on them. The rice and noodles were good. But when a $19 serve of fried chicken has less meat in it than a standard drumstick would, the good aspects …

author: Jordon Gan
text: Paik's Noodle, otherwise known as Hong Kong Banjeom 0410 is a usual spot I go to over the years whenever I crave for Korean Chinese food and am easily familiar with their signature Jajjangmyeon (자짱면), Jjamppong (짬뽕) and Tangsuyuk (탕수육) which to me is quintessential Korean Chinese dishes. On this occasion, I came on Thursday night with my brother where we dined on the second floor and feasted on Jajjangmyeon, Gochu (Chilli) Jajjangmyeon, small serve of Tangsuyuk, Chilli Prawns and their fried egg, with everything being mouthwatering, especially both the Jajjangmyeon and Tangsuyuk. This meal was the first time I tried the Chilli Prawns however they were a letdown as it wasn't what i was expecting being not so drenched with the chilli sauce. Nevertheless an affordable meal overall. Nevertheless I love the restaurant's atmosphere which has an excellent playlist of K-Pop songs, from old to new! A restaurant worth visiting if you're looking for Korean Chinese food.

She also handed us new water without us asking) Foods (we had too wait a bit long but the food was worth the wait!) - Jajang-myeon Noodle (Korean black bean sauces noodles with stir-fried pork and vegetables) (13,5$): 9/10 - Tang-suyuk (Deep-fried pork served with assorted vegetables in sweet and sour sauce (21,8$): 9/10 Water: 10/10 (Cold, ready water)

author: Josep Kim
text: The food was good. Honestly, I'd probably come here instead of going to Box Hill or Clayton. But the service was a bit disappointing. The food came out quite slowly, and the timing was off. I only got the sweet and sour pork (tangsooyook) after I'd already eaten more than half of my jajangmyeon. What was really unacceptable was that they gave me just one tiny piece of onion and three pieces of pickled radish… The food tasted okay. I'd probably come here instead of going to Box Hill or Clayton. The service was a bit disappointing, though. The food was a bit slow to come out, and the timing wasn't perfect, so I only got the tangsuyuk after eating more than half of the jajangmyeon. The real deal was that they only served one piece of onion and three pieces of danmuji; that was unbearable.

author: Sam Lan
text: I was extremely disappointed with the food. Ordered skewers, tangsuhyuk and black bean noodles. The black bean noodles had a good texture. However, the dish came out lukewarm and the sauce was not tasty. The tangsuhyuk (fried pork) was also cold/lukewarm and not crispy. And the skewers were so completely different to what other skewer chains and even Hu fried skewers usually sell. Barely fried and not crispy at all. The rice cakes had oyster sauce brushed on top? The cheese ball tasted boiled. The sign said takoyaki ball, but when I ate it, it was boiled and it was something like a cuttlefish ball. False advertising. The skewers were a major letdown considering we spent so much money on them. Eating oily foods is only enjoyable when they're served hot and deep fried. Nobody wants to eat cold and boiled fried dishes. At this price point, I wish I went to a different restaurant. Staff were friendly.

author: Autumn U

author: Yuka Monica
text: Authentic Korean noodles with bold flavours and generous portions! Tried the Jajangmyeon, Jjamppong, and Tangsuyuk all incredibly tasty and satisfying. The noodles were perfectly chewy, sauces rich and savoury, and the crispy Tangsuyuk was a highlight 🤤 Service was friendly and quick, and the restaurant has a clean, modern vibe both inside and outdoors. Definitely worth the visit if you’re craving affordable and delicious Korean comfort food and yes, they’re open 7 days a week! Will definitely come back!

We ordered the lunch special, Jajangmyeon/Tansuyuk combo. The look of it was good until we tasted it. Start with the Tansuyuk, it's one of my favourite but this was not crispy (more like soggy) and the sauce wasn't even hot (more like luke warm). The Jajangmyeon was ok but nothing special and I have had better one than this. Overall, we were so disappointed and it's not even worth coming back.

author: Justin Tan
text: I decided to check out this venue after reading the reviews here. I have a combination meal consisting of their signature black bean noodle and a portion of the sweet and sour pork. The black bean sauce is quite bland and dilute, different from the more salty version in Cantonese cuisine. The noodles remind me of buckwheat noodles, are chewy in texture. Overall, this dish is acceptable. Despite raving reviews of their sweet and sour pork, I found that their batter is chewy, suggesting that the batter must have contained a tapioca or a sweet potato starch. This chewiness compromises the crispness that is typical of Chinese-style sweet and sour pork. This is certainly a Korean version. They may consider offering the dipping sauce separately instead of pouring it over the pork. The venue imposes a 10% surcharge on Sundays, which may explain why it wasn't busy. This is an average experience for me, and I suspect that the other reviewers of this venue may have received a special inducement by the owners to write something favourable. I paid this meal myself.

author: Sparkey
text: Honestly nothing special. Wanted to try this, for a while, but was let down sadly. It's basically a fast food chain version of jajangmyeon, and there are plenty of smaller family owned businesses near by serving up better service, better portions, and better flavours. The spicy version of Jajangmyeon was just throwing a ton of raw chiles on top, and it was enough to zap the taste buds and kill the unique blackbean flavours. Jjamppong was decent, but same applies, nothing special. In a pinch, on a tight timeline, or just need something convenient, then this serves a purpose, but don't go out of your way to try this, you will be let down.

author: DY Kim
text: Koreans, if you only go to Hong Kong Banjeom and come to Ams, you're in trouble. I've been here twice, and the first time, the jjambbong, gochujang jjambbong, and jjambbong rice all tasted different. Still, the jjajangmyeon was edible, and I remember the tangsuyuk being delicious. I visited again yesterday after four months, and the taste has gotten even worse. The jjambbong has too much of the raw chili powder flavor, and the jjajangmyeon, which was decent, lacks depth and has a strong soybean paste flavor. Overall, the depth of flavor is significantly lower than in Korea, so there's a lot of room for improvement!
